### Next Atlanta Streets Alive: Sunday, May 31, 2026 – West End to Grant Park (Ralph David Abernathy Blvd. to Georgia Ave.)

![Atlanta_Streets_Alive_Peachtree_2016-93.jpg](https://d3n8a8pro7vhmx.cloudfront.net/atlbike/pages/1200/attachments/original/1519238455/Atlanta_Streets_Alive_Peachtree_2016-93.jpg?1519238455)

![asa shield](https://d3n8a8pro7vhmx.cloudfront.net/atlbike/pages/1200/attachments/original/1457584740/ASA_Logo_800px.png?1457584740)

### **2026 Atlanta Streets Alive Planned Dates:**  Sunday, March 22, 2026 – MLK: Beltline to Broad (new route)  Sunday, April 19, 2026 – Peachtree St. (15th St. to Alabama St.)  Sunday, May 31, 2026 – West End to Grant Park (Ralph David Abernathy Blvd. to Georgia Ave.)  Sunday, June 14, 2026 – MLK: Beltline to Vine (new route) (FIFA-related)  Sunday, September 27, 2026 – Peachtree St. (15th St. to Alabama St.)  Sunday, October 18, 2026 – West End to Grant Park (Ralph David Abernathy Blvd. to Georgia Ave.)  Sunday, November 8, 2026 – Howell Mill Rd. (Westside Stride Partnership)    _\*ASA dates during FIFA may change as needed_    Atlanta Streets Alive opens streets temporarily for constituents to bike, walk, skate, dance, and play. It's free to the public; the event turns the street into a park space that connects our city! Atlanta Streets Alive is part of an international open streets movement happening in many cities throughout the U.S., Canada, and around the world. [Read more](https://www.atlantastreetsalive.org/about).

**Thank you to our sponsors!**

### [Interactive Map: Peachtree St - April 19, 2026](https://www.arcgis.com/apps/dashboards/5f1ac22709954cc18c46d2d9ece23c24\#Route=Peachtree)

ArcGIS Dashboards

an initiative of the [Atlanta Department of Transportation](https://atldot.atlantaga.gov/) with
support by [Propel ATL](http://www.letspropelatl.org/) and the Atlanta community.



Join us as we open streets for people. Get ready to experience your community streets in a safer, healthier, more livable way. Atlanta Streets Alive was created by Propel ATL, then known as the Atlanta Bicycle Coalition, in 2010. The initiative went on hiatus in 2020 due to the pandemic. In 2023 Atlanta Streets Alive returned as an official City of Atlanta program with support from Propel ATL and other stakeholders.

#### **Interested in sponsoring Atlanta Streets Alive or have questions?**

Contact [RAMaloon@atlantaga.gov](mailto:RAMaloon@atlantaga.gov)

* * *

Get Propel ATL updates

Sign in with [email](https://www.atlantastreetsalive.org/login)

Developed by the [Propel ATL (formerly the Atlanta Bicycle\\
Coalition and PEDS)](http://www.letspropelatl.org/) and [Lauren Langley](http://www.laurenlangleydesign.com/) with [NationBuilder.](http://nationbuilder.com/)
Photos by [Steve Eberhardt.](https://www.flickr.com/photos/steveeberhardt/page1)

Twitter Widget Iframe

metadata.ogDescription: A monthly Open Street presented by ATLDOT with support by Propel ATL & the Atlanta community. Returning to Peachtree Street: fall 2023! Atlanta Streets Alive opens streets for people by taking cars out of the equation for an afternoon to create a whole new healthy, sustainable and vibrant city street experience. Atlanta Streets Alive is a program inspired by open streets projects all over the world. Bring your bicycle or just your feet, and get ready to explore your community streets in a safer, healthier, more livable way.
